creator: Richard.Frewin@astro.cf.ac.uk
text:
On AMD64 audio/grip crashes with a segmentation fault about a second after the main window appears on screen.  Tested on both 8.0-RC1 and 8.1-STABLE (from Sep 11 2010), both with up-to-date ports trees.  Happens as both non-root and root user.  However, this seg fault does not seem to happen on i386 systems (tested 8.1-STABLE and 9.0-CURRENT).

GDB shows the following ...

birch> gdb grip
GNU gdb 6.1.1 [FreeBSD]
[...]
(gdb) run
[...]
Program received signal SIGSEGV, Segmentation fault.
[Switching to Thread 8074041c0 (LWP 100267)]
0x0000000802ef7750 in gtk_widget_set_parent ()
   from /usr/X11R6/lib/libgtk-x11-2.0.so.0
(gdb) bt
#0  0x0000000802ef7750 in gtk_widget_set_parent ()
   from /usr/X11R6/lib/libgtk-x11-2.0.so.0
#1  0x0000000802dde9ee in gtk_image_menu_item_set_image ()
   from /usr/X11R6/lib/libgtk-x11-2.0.so.0
#2  0x000000000041cbb7 in ?? ()
#3  0x0000000000422aba in ?? ()

Looking at the source code this appears to be called from BuildMenuItemXpm at grip-3.3.1/src/uihelper.c:104 .  I'm not a coder and can't see the actual cause if the crash.
